name: Grilled Pork Tacos & Papaya Salsa
recipeIngredient: 1 papaya, peeled, seeded, and cut into 1/2 inch cubes
1 small red chili, seeded, and finely chopped
1/4 pound pork (boneless center loin), cut into strips measuring 2 by 1/4 inch
10 flour tortillas (6 or 7 inches in diameter), warmed
1 1/2 cups shredded Monterey Jack cheese (6 ounces)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook the pork in a 10-inch skillet over medium heat for about 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until no longer pink and drained.
2. Stir in the chopped papaya and pineapple.
3. Preheat the oven to 425°F.
4. Spoon about 1/4 cup of the pork mixture onto half of each tortilla.
5. Top with about 2 tablespoons of shredded cheese.
6. Fold the tortillas over the filling.
7. Arrange five of the filled tortillas in an ungreased jelly roll pan, measuring 15 1/2 by 10 1/2 by 1 inch, and brush with melted margarine.
8. Bake uncovered for about 10 minutes or until lightly golden brown. Repeat with the remaining tacos.
9. Serve with Papaya Salsa. Makes 5 servings.
Salsa: Mix all ingredients. Cover and refrigerate for about 30 minutes or until chilled. Makes 3 cups salsa.
